reasoning: The current baseline mortgage index is approximately 5.693% (as of Jan 16). The 10-year Treasury yield, a strong leading indicator for mortgage rates, has stabilized in the 4.13%-4.18% range over mid-January. The FRED statistical tool shows conflicting signals: a short-term downward linear trend over the last month (predicting 5.646% by Jan 25, implying a ~34% chance of increase), but recent seasonal momentum pointing to a ~67% chance of a seasonal uptick. Given this, the baseline statistical projection sits exactly at 50%. Looking ahead, the Federal Reserve is heavily favored (83% probability) to hold rates steady at its late January meeting, which removes immediate catalysts for steep drops but may limit rallies as well since 2026 expectations generally price in 1-2 cuts. Taking the slightly downward drift in yields into account, I estimate a 48% chance of the index strictly increasing by Jan 25. For the longer horizon (Feb 17), uncertainty regresses slightly towards 50%, making it 49%.
